items.title: Councillor vows to keep working despite suspension for misconduct

children.content: Ashleigh Vandenberg, a councillor in Melbourne's outer-west, plans to continue in her role, despite being handed a two-month suspension for misconduct.

gallery.altText: A woman with long brown hair wearing a patterned top.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long brown hair wearing a patterned top.

items.title: Victim-survivor 'devastated' by Christian Brothers compensation shortfall

children.content: The Christian Brothers order plans to sell off its remaining assets to fund compensation claims to abuse survivors but admits it will not be enough to cover the amount owed.

image.altText: A photo of a young teenage boy, clearly an old photo

items.title: IVF add-ons are common in Australia, but there's no proof most work

children.content: Experts urge clinics and specialists to "carefully consider" offering IVF add-on treatments as they can be seen as "implicit endorsement of benefit" when there's little evidence most work.

image.altText: A composite image of a woman smiling with a needle and a woman taking a selfie.

items.title: Future unclear for Mt Buller school that trained Winter Olympians

children.content: A snow school on Mount Buller faces an uncertain future following a government decision not to open it for enrolments this winter.

image.altText: A family with their son on the mountain in the snow

items.title: 'Confusopoly' blamed as power bill messages befuddle consumers

children.content: Some electricity users have been hit with higher charges, despite all the talk of the lower default market offers. Retailers say a rebalancing of supply and usage charges is the cause, but there are signs some are clawing back profit by raising their cheapest plans.

image.altText: A graphic image of a power plug overlaid on a house and electricity bill

items.title: Kids Helpline crisis interventions on the rise again towards COVID high

children.content: Kids Helpline says a growing number of Australian young people are requiring immediate emergency help, with a concerning trend around crisis interventions.

gallery.altText: Girl sitting down with her hands clasped.

thumbnail.altText: Girl sitting down with her hands clasped.

items.title: Daryl Braithwaite announces end to live performances

children.content: Australian vocalist, former Sherbet frontman and ARIA Hall of Fame inductee Daryl Braithwaite says he is stepping back from singing live.

image.altText: Daryl Braithwaite on ARIA Hall of Fame status and why he still loves performing

items.title: Church youth leader sentenced for sexually assaulting 13-year-old

children.content: A man who assaulted a girl in his car has dodged a lengthy jail term, with a judge saying there were "exceptional circumstances" that warranted a discounted sentence.

image.altText: Joshua Emonson poses for a photo with four other people

items.title: Police locate body believed to be missing Victorian man

children.content: Police say the body of a male was located during the search for Victorian man Neville Nicholson who has been missing since last Wednesday.

image.altText: A video frame from CCTV footage of the last known sighting of missing a Horsham man

items.title: Australia approves restricted use of herbicide banned in 70 other countries

children.content: The Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority concludes its nearly 30-year review of the controversial but popular herbicide.

image.altText: A label on a plastic tub reads CORROSIVE LIQUID, TOXIC N.O.S. PARAQUAT DICHLORIDE

items.title: Emergency alerts and warnings still lost on multicultural communities

children.content: Multicultural communities may not automatically understand the intent behind Australia's emergency messaging, but there are ways to fix this.

gallery.altText: A blonde white woman and a black woman in a maroon hijab walk together down the street, laughing.

thumbnail.altText: A blonde white woman and a black woman in a maroon hijab walk together down the street, laughing.

items.title: Potent sedative found in vapes sold in Melbourne

children.content: Victorian health authorities say vape products containing the powerful sedative etomidate have been found being sold illegally.

image.altText: An illegal vape.

items.title: Victorian farm fined for not meeting free-range standards

children.content: Doreen Egg admits it sold eggs labelled as "free range" despite its hens not having access to an outdoor range, as required under Free Range Egg Labelling Information Standard.

image.altText: A cluster of hens crowded towards the camera, blue sky and grass behind.

items.title: Christian Brothers 'squib' on abuse compensation, lawyer says

children.content: The Catholic order says it will sell off its remaining assets to fund abuse compensation, but the money raised is expected to fall short of what is owed to victims.

image.altText: Cross on top of a church

items.title: Calls to upgrade 'sketchy' Sturt Highway freight route

children.content: Eight councils are calling for urgent safety upgrades to the popular freight and travel route spanning three states.

image.altText: Sturt Highway city distance sign

items.title: Buyers stuck in contracts for ambitious skyscraper that isn’t being built

children.content: Apartment buyers who were sold on ambitious plans for the tallest building in the country say they're facing up to a decade of uncertainty after the head-turning project faltered.

gallery.altText: a mock up image of what the tower would look like when complete.
a woman wearing a black jacket with brown hair.

thumbnail.altText: a mock up image of what the tower would look like when complete.
a woman wearing a black jacket with brown hair.

items.title: Logging company gives new answer to parliament over logs sent to Victoria

children.content: Sustainable Timber Tasmania has corrected the record after earlier stating that all logs from public native forests are processed in Tasmania.

image.altText: A semi-trailer loaded with logs in a traffic lane approaching a port. The large Spirit of Tasmania ship is in front of it.

items.title: High cost of living fuels record regional migration, report says

children.content: The rate at which capital city dwellers are moving the regions is at its highest level on record, which the report's author says shows the trend is more than a post-COVID blip.

image.altText: Aerial view of sailing boats on a water with a city as a backdrop

items.title: Police release CCTV as part of probe into former boxer's death

children.content: Police are searching for a sedan they believe was used to get away from the scene of a fatal shooting in Melbourne's inner north-west.

image.altText: Several vehicles driving near tram tracks at an intersection at night with street lights

items.title: Community waits for verdict as GrainCorp class action wraps up

children.content: A class trial against GrainCorp over the alleged impact of its canola processing factory in Victoria's north on the local community has wrapped up.

image.altText: An older man in a grey shirt and sunglasses walks towards court.

items.title: Officers open up about horror 2020 Melbourne freeway tragedy

children.content: The deaths of four Victoria Police officers on April 22, 2020 sent shock waves through the force, with the impacts of Eastern Freeway tragedy still felt six years on.

items.title: Hunters and wildlife rescuers share concerns about urban sprawl near lake

children.content: Geelong's population boom has brought housing into close proximity with duck hunting.

image.altText: Man in camoflague wades through lake with suburban houses in the background

items.title: 'ISIS bride' to live 'peaceful existence' if granted bail, court hears

children.content: Charged with four slavery-related offences, Kawsar Ahmad is seeking bail in a multi-day hearing in the Melbourne Magistrates' Court.

image.altText: A woman wearing a brown hijab sits in court.

items.title: Victoria ended native forest logging. So where are these trees coming from?

children.content: Four Corners follows the logs, and the money, to uncover how efforts to end logging have shifted the timber trade across state borders.

image.altText: A photo shot through the windscreen of a car, showing a truck loaded with logs ahead on a semi-suburban street.

items.title: Sydney Swan Taylor Adams announces AFL retirement

children.content: Hard-luck Sydney midfielder Taylor Adams has called time on his AFL career after a tough run with soft-tissue injuries.

image.altText: AFL player Taylor Adams smiling, holding his right arm aloft in celebration

items.title: French soccer coach using Duolingo to help train his Aussie team

children.content: He may not know the language, but French-speaking William Loison is embracing his position coaching a regional Victorian soccer team with the help of a popular language app.

gallery.altText: A man holds a soccer ball
Thirteen soccer players posing for a photo at a soccer ground
Close up of a young soccer player in front of a soccer goal

thumbnail.altText: A man holds a soccer ball
Thirteen soccer players posing for a photo at a soccer ground
Close up of a young soccer player in front of a soccer goal

items.title: Colleen Harkin quits Liberal Party to join One Nation

children.content: Liberal Party member and think tank education director Colleen Harkin has quit the party a week after attending a One Nation fundraiser in Melbourne.

image.altText: Colleen Harkin wears a pink blazer

items.title: Auction clearance rates fall to lowest level in six years

children.content: Not since the early days of the pandemic have sellers seen such disappointing results at auction, with Cotality predicting "a further loss of momentum" in the housing market.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit gesticulates at an auction with a house behind

items.title: Australian wool is 'back in vogue' as the sector experiences a resurgence

children.content: A strong wool price is inspiring optimism among young professionals as the industry bounces back.

gallery.altText: A collage of photos of a woman posing in a variety of woolen clothing

thumbnail.altText: A collage of photos of a woman posing in a variety of woolen clothing

items.title: Bells Beach temporarily closed due to whale carcass washing up

children.content: Victoria's world-famous Bells Beach has been temporarily closed after a whale carcass washed on to shore earlier today.

image.altText: A whale carcass has washed up on Victoria's Bells Beach.

items.title: Family concerned over disappearance of 47-year-old Victorian man

children.content: The family of Horsham man Neville Nicholson is concerned for his safety and is desperate for his safe return.

image.altText: neville and family

items.title: Ticketless travel for Melbourne trams has arrived

children.content: From today, Melbourne tram commuters can use their credit card, phone or smartwatch to get around.

image.altText: Close up of a commuter's hand tapping their myki card on a tram fare card reading machine

items.title: Bird flu detection 'a distant concern' for Victoria, industry says

children.content: The head of Victoria's peak farming group says the detection of the deadly H5 variant of avian influenza in Western Australia is a "distant concern" for Victorians.

image.altText: Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

items.title: Melbourne woman suffers acute liver failure after using knock-off drug

children.content: Megan Hancocks bought a drug labelled as the peptide compound retatrutide in cash from a beautician. Within a week of her second dose, she was in acute liver failure.

gallery.altText: A woman lies in a hospital bed with her eyes closed.

thumbnail.altText: A woman lies in a hospital bed with her eyes closed.

items.title: Socceroos live events marred by flares, alleged sexual assault

children.content: Thousands of people flocked to sites across Australia to show their support as the Socceroos fall short against World Cup host USA.
